title: Learning React
subtitle: Modern Patterns for Developing React Apps
author: Alex Banks, Eve Porcello
themes: Hooks as the modern React paradigm
State management from local to global
excerpt: A hands-on guide to building web applications with React and its ecosystem — covering components, hooks, state management, routing, and GraphQL integration.
keyIdeas: State management with useState, useReducer, and Context API
keyTakeaways: React is a library, not a framework — choose companions carefully
Hooks have replaced class components as the standard
whoShouldRead: Web developers transitioning from jQuery or Angular
Full-stack developers adding React to their toolkit
Anyone modernizing a legacy frontend codebase
whoShouldSkip: Experienced React developers seeking advanced patterns
Complete programming beginners — need JavaScript fundamentals first
Those looking for TypeScript-specific React guidance
